sn47.20:1.2: that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying among the Sumbhas. Now there is a Sumbhan town named Sedaka.
sn47.20:1.3: There the Blessed One addressed the monks,
sn47.20:1.5: “Yes, lord,” the monks responded to him.
sn47.20:2.1: “Suppose, monks, that a large crowd of people were to come thronging together, saying, ‘The beauty queen! The beauty queen!’
sn47.20:2.2: And suppose that the beauty queen were highly accomplished at singing & dancing,
sn47.20:2.3: so that an even greater crowd would come thronging, saying, ‘The beauty queen is singing! The beauty queen is dancing!’
sn47.20:2.4: Then a man would come along, desiring life & shrinking from death, desiring pleasure & abhorring pain.
sn47.20:2.6: ‘Now look here, mister. You must take this bowl filled to the brim with oil and carry it on your head in between the great crowd & the beauty queen.
sn47.20:2.7: A man with a raised sword will follow right behind you,
sn47.20:2.8: and wherever you spill even a drop of oil, right there will he cut off your head.’
sn47.20:2.9: Now what do you think, monks?
sn47.20:2.10: Would that man, not paying attention to the bowl of oil, let himself get distracted outside?”
sn47.20:3.1: “I have given you this parable to convey a meaning.
sn47.20:3.3: The bowl filled to the brim with oil stands for mindfulness immersed in the body.
sn47.20:3.5: ‘We will develop mindfulness immersed in the body. We will pursue it, hand it the reins, take it as a basis, steady it, consolidate it, and undertake it well.’
sn47.20:3.6: That is how you should train yourselves.”
